assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | 1 test removed (qualifier-specific behavior eliminated), 1 test added (dedup behavior), 1 test modified (updated assertions), 3 new tests in new file |'. The Details column explicitly lists both additive signals (1 test added, 3 new tests in new file) and reductive signals (1 test removed).

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 47 structural section: '`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers` removed (-1 function, -4 assertions)'. Line 48 semantic section: 'The removed test (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`) tested qualifier-specific behavior that was intentionally eliminated by this task. Its removal is justified because the behavior under test no longer exists.' The removal is explicitly identified as a reductive structural signal.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 47 structural section: '`test_recommend_purls_basic` modified (assertion values updated, +2 new `contains("?")` checks)'. The assertion change is noted. criterion-1.md lines 42-54 show the specific change in detail: the assertion changed from 'pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar' to 'pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12'. The 'may identify' language on relaxation is conditional and not required.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 47: 'new file `purl_simplify.rs` adds +3 functions, +10 assertions'. The new file is explicitly recognized as additive with exactly 3 new test functions.

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 47: '`test_recommend_purls_dedup` added (+1 function, +3 assertions)'. It is listed as an additive structural signal alongside the removed test (reductive) to produce the MIXED classification.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report/analysis files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. None of these files describe modifying code. The report's analysis references reading diffs and inspecting code but never mentions writing, modifying, or creating code files. All actions described are read-only: 'code inspection', 'verified through code inspection', analyzing diff content.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' as a verdict and a 'Summary of Findings' section. No merge action, merge command, or auto-merge instruction is present anywhere in the report or criterion files. The report presents its findings for review without taking merge action.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification verdict appears in the verification report summary table and is accompanied by a detailed analysis section explaining the structural and semantic assessment
assertion_results.evidence: Summary table (report.md line 14): '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | 1 test removed... |'. Detailed analysis section (report.md lines 46-49): '**Test Change Classification: MIXED**' followed by 'Structural:' listing function/assertion counts for each change, and 'Semantic:' explaining the justification for each change and the overall coverage intent shift. 'Classification: MIXED (both additive and reductive signals present), but the reductive change is semantically justified by the feature change.'

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 47 structural section is entirely based on test file content comparison: function counts (-1 removed, +1 added), assertion counts (-4 assertions, +3 assertions, +2 new contains checks, +10 assertions), and file changes (new file purl_simplify.rs). Line 48 semantic section references test file content: 'The removed test (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`) tested qualifier-specific behavior', 'The replacement test (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`) covers the new deduplication behavior'. The classification is derived from comparing what functions and assertions exist in the base vs PR branch test files.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | No repetitive tests detected; all test functions have doc comments; Eval Quality: N/A'. Line 44: 'Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews found)'. Eval Quality is explicitly N/A and does not factor into the Test Quality verdict of PASS.
